auth_module_map.role: Defines AbstractBaseUser (lines 43-164) with core auth fields: password (CharField, line 44), last_login (DateTimeField, line 45); BaseUserManager (lines 21-40) provides get_by_natural_key, normalize_email utilities

Defines AbstractUser (lines 446-514) extending AbstractBaseUser + PermissionsMixin; includes username (CharField, line 456), first_name (CharField, line 468), last_name (CharField, line 469), email (EmailField, line 470), is_staff (BooleanField, line 471), is_active (BooleanField, line 476), date_joined (DateTimeField, line 484); User (lines 517-526) is concrete proxy

Defines PermissionsMixin (lines 317-443) providing groups (ManyToManyField, line 331) and user_permissions (ManyToManyField, line 342) with permission checking methods (has_perm, has_module_perms, etc.)
UnicodeUsernameValidator (lines 19-25) validates username format; used in AbstractUser.username field definition (models.py:456)

UserCreationForm (lines 137+), UserChangeForm (lines 228+), AdminUserCreationForm, AdminPasswordChangeForm integrate with AbstractUser fields; expects username, email, password, first_name, last_name, is_staff, is_active, groups, user_permissions
UserAdmin class (lines 23+) configures admin fieldsets for User model; expects built-in fields (username, email, password, first_name, last_name, is_staff, is_active, date_joined)

Initial migration schema: User model with id (AutoField, line 86), password (CharField, line 93), last_login (DateTimeField, line 96), is_superuser (BooleanField, line 102), username (CharField + UnicodeUsernameValidator, lines 113-121), first_name (CharField, line 126), last_name (CharField, line 132), email (EmailField, line 138), is_staff (BooleanField, line 144), is_active (BooleanField, line 154), date_joined (DateTimeField, line 165)

migration_trace: Step 1: Create new User model extending AbstractBaseUser (not AbstractUser) in app/models.py; define custom fields (profile_tier, external_sso_id, last_sync_at) inline

Step 2: Create UserManager extending BaseUserManager (django/contrib/auth/base_user.py:21-40) with _create_user, create_user, create_superuser methods; override to set custom fields with defaults (e.g., profile_tier='free', external_sso_id=None, last_sync_at=None)

Step 3: Create PermissionsMixin integration by manually inheriting or composing Permission/Group models; inherit from django.contrib.auth.models.PermissionsMixin (lines 317-443) to get groups, user_permissions ManyToManyFields and permission checking methods (has_perm, has_perms, has_module_perms)
Step 4: Set AUTH_USER_MODEL='app.User' in settings.py before running migrations

Step 5: Create initial migration with CreateModel for custom User with all fields (built-in: password, last_login, username, email, first_name, last_name, is_staff, is_active, date_joined; custom: profile_tier CharField(choices), external_sso_id CharField(unique=True, null=True), last_sync_at DateTimeField(null=True)); add M2M fields for groups and user_permissions linking to auth.Group and auth.Permission

Step 6: Update forms (inherit UserCreationForm from django/contrib/auth/forms.py:137+, UserChangeForm from lines 228+; override fields property to include custom fields; add clean_<field> validators for custom fields)
Step 7: Update admin (inherit UserAdmin; override fieldsets to include custom fields in appropriate sections; see django/contrib/auth/admin.py:23+)
Step 8: Run migrations: ensure auth app migrations run first (populate Group, Permission), then app migrations (create custom User table with M2M relations)
Step 9: Create data migration if existing User data needs transformation (e.g., backfill profile_tier='free' for existing users)

Consider adding signal handler or model default for automatic initialization
